- Useful contacts: Have the contacts of local authorities and emergency services at hand.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker using the provincial road of Grantola without an alternative plan could face delays of up to three hours.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker who has identified alternative routes and uses navigation apps could reduce delays to less than an hour.

#### Regulations and Rules
- Road traffic regulations: Introduction of stricter controls for heavy vehicles, with fines up to 5,000 Swiss francs for serious violations.
- Road safety plans: Implementation of specific road safety plans for mountainous areas, with an investment of 2 million Swiss francs in the next three years.

#### Concrete Examples
- Municipality of Grantola: Installation of new safety barriers along the provincial road, with a cost of 500,000 Swiss francs.
- Municipality of Brogeda: Increase in the frequency of checks on heavy vehicles, with an investment of 300,000 Swiss francs.

In conclusion, the incident in Grantola underscores the importance of improving road infrastructure and adopting preventive measures to avoid similar road blockages in the future.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). ## Actions to Take
For cross-border workers and drivers using the Grantola provincial road, it is crucial to follow some guidelines to avoid inconveniences and ensure safety, especially in scenarios like the truck incident that blocked the road while waiting for the recovery vehicle.

### Emergency Procedure
1. **Get Real-Time Information**: Use real-time traffic applications like Google Maps or Waze to know the road conditions and any possible blockages. For example, during the Grantola incident, these apps reported an average delay of 45 minutes for vehicles heading towards Lugano.
2. Plan Alternative Routes: Identify alternative routes in advance, such as the SS36 of San Gottardo or the SS39 of San Bernardino, which can avoid delays in case of accidents. For those traveling from Varese to Lugano, an alternative route might add about 20 kilometers but significantly reduce waiting times.
3. Follow Road Signs: Follow the instructions of the fire department and local authorities. During the incident, the police diverted traffic to the SS36, reducing congestion in Grantola.

### Useful Tools
To best plan your trips, cross-border workers can use tools like the [traffic calculator](nav:traffic-history) and the [route comparator](nav:border-map) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. These tools allow you to monitor traffic conditions and find alternative routes if necessary. For example, the route comparator can show that an alternative route from Mendrisio to Lugano might be 15 minutes longer but completely avoid blocked traffic.

### Operational Checklist
- Before Departure:
 - Check real-time traffic conditions.
 - Plan alternative routes.
 - Ensure the vehicle is in good condition, especially in winter when mountain roads can be dangerous.
- During the Trip:
 - Follow road signs and authorities' instructions.
 - Maintain a safe distance from vehicles ahead, especially in heavy traffic.
- In Case of Emergency:
 - Use the route comparator to find alternative routes.
 - Contact local authorities for real-time updates.

### Comparisons of Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker traveling from Varese to Lugano every day can save up to 30 minutes a day by using alternative routes during road blockages.
- Scenario 2: During winter, mountain roads like the SS36 can be closed due to snow. In these cases, it is essential to have a plan B, such as using the SS39 of San Bernardino, which is less subject to sudden closures.

### Conclusion
The incident in Grantola is a reminder of the importance of being prepared and informed when traveling in mountainous areas. For cross-border workers, planning alternative routes and using traffic monitoring tools can make the difference between a hassle-free trip and a stressful experience. Staying updated on road conditions and following authorities' instructions is fundamental to ensuring everyone's safety.

[Learn more about services for cross-border workers](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/29/leggi-notizia/argomenti/cronaca-20/articolo/foto-camion-incastrato-in-un-tornante-sulla-provinciale-a-grantola-strada-bloccata-in-attesa-dell.html)*

item.title: Ticino-Gaza twinning exhibition
item.description: An exhibition in Viganello tells the story of the twinning between Ticinese and Gaza students, a Future in Peace project for human rights education.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students in Viganello
- Future in Peace project for human rights education
- Exhibition until May 7, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Exhibition on the twinning between schools in Viganello and Gaza
- When: From April 27 to May 7, 2026
- Where: Viganello Middle School, via Boscioro 7
- Who: Future in Peace association and Viganello Middle School
- Amount: Not yet specified

The project, exhibited at Viganello Middle School until May 7, 2026, 'has directly connected young people living in deeply different realities.' This is how the note from the Future in Peace association, titled 'Two schools, two worlds, one bond: an exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students,' begins. The exhibition presents drawings, letters, and testimonies from the students, offering the public a direct, authentic, and, at times, unsettling glimpse into this encounter between two worlds. Opening hours: Monday to Friday, during school hours.

### The twinning project
The project, born from the collaboration between the Future in Peace association and Italian teacher Alessandro Fanciotti, has directly connected young people living in deeply different realities. Through the exchange of letters, drawings, and personal stories, the students did not just communicate: they recognized each other, sharing dreams, fears, and hopes.

For the boys and girls of Gaza, the twinning represented much more than a school exchange. It was a moment of respite, a fragment of normality in a life marked by deprivation and uncertainty. Speaking with peers from the other side of the world gave them a sensation often denied: that of being seen, heard, and considered. In a context where everything seems to be collapsing, even just knowing that someone thinks of them has reignited a spark of joy.

For the students of Viganello, the impact was just as profound. Confronting such a harsh reality meant looking at their own lives with new eyes. They discovered what it means to live without certainties, without stability, without the things that are taken for granted for them: a safe home, a family protected, a school where they can learn every day. This confrontation generated awareness, gratitude, and a form of maturity that no book can teach.

### The value of education
At the heart of the exhibition is the value of education as a fundamental right. In Gaza, access to school is today severely compromised: bombings have destroyed not only school buildings but also the continuity of young people's dreams. Al-Salam School, created and supported by Future in Peace, was born precisely for this reason: to offer a space where one can still dream.

This project, explains Sofia Alberti, Co-president of Future in Peace, quoted in the note, 'demonstrates how important it is, even for young people in Switzerland, to confront different realities to develop awareness, empathy, and responsibility. At the same time, it shows that children, even in the most difficult contexts, continue to live and hope, just like every child. Our task is to protect not only their survival but also their right to imagine a future.' - ### Implications for Education and Society
The twinning project between students from Ticino and Gaza represents a concrete example of how education can build bridges between different cultures and realities. This type of initiative not only enriches the educational path of the students involved but also contributes to creating a more aware and supportive society.

- ### Comparing Different Realities
For the students of Viganello, the comparison with the reality of Gaza has represented a unique opportunity to develop empathy and understanding towards life situations very different from theirs. This type of educational experience can have a lasting impact on their worldview and their future commitment to social and humanitarian causes.

- ### Supporting Education in Crisis Contexts
The project highlights the importance of supporting education even in crisis contexts. The Al-Salam school, created and supported by Future in Peace, is an example of how it is possible to offer quality education even in situations of great difficulty. This type of initiative can inspire other organizations and individuals to support similar projects in other parts of the world.

- ### The Role of Associations and Schools
Associations like Future in Peace and local schools play a fundamental role in promoting twinning and cultural exchange projects. These projects not only enrich the educational path of the students but also contribute to creating a more open and supportive society. Schools can integrate these projects into their curriculum, offering students unique opportunities for learning and personal growth.

- ### Opportunities for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers living and working in Ticino can greatly benefit from initiatives like this. Participating in twinning and cultural exchange projects can enrich their life experience in Switzerland, offering them the opportunity to learn about new cultures and develop new skills. Furthermore, these projects can contribute to creating a more cohesive and supportive community, where all members can feel valued and respected.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### How to Participate in the Exhibition
To participate in the exhibition on the twinning between Ticino and Gaza students, you can visit the Viganello middle school, located at via Boscioro 7, from Monday to Friday during school hours. The exhibition will be open to the public until May 7, 2026.

### Contacts and Information
For further information on the project and the Future in Peace association, you can visit the association's official website or directly contact the Viganello middle school. The Future in Peace association is committed to promoting human rights and international justice, and regularly organizes events and initiatives to raise public awareness on these issues.

### Useful Tools
To further explore the topic and discover similar initiatives, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities in Ticino and Italy. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful for those who wish to move to Switzerland and want to know the requirements and necessary procedures.

### Conclusion
The twinning project between Ticino and Gaza students represents a concrete example of how education can build bridges between different cultures and realities. Participating in initiatives like this can enrich the educational path of students and contribute to creating a more aware and supportive society. For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, these opportunities can represent a unique way to get to know new cultures and develop new skills, enriching their life experience in Switzerland.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/due-scuole-due-mondi-un-solo-legame-una-mostra-sul-gemellaggio-tra-studenti-ticinesi-e-di-gaza-432491)*

item.title: Vergiate turns colorful: the non-competitive Color Run returns for all ages
item.description: On Sunday, May 24, 2026, Vergiate hosts the Color Run, a 4 km race open to all ages. Registrations open until May 10.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Non-competitive 4 km run in Vergiate
- Open to all ages
- Registrations until May 10th
- Cost: 10 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Color Run, non-competitive run
- When: Sunday, May 24, 2026
- Where: Vergiate, Italy
- Who: Organized by Oratorio San Giovanni Bosco with the patronage of the Municipality
- Fee: Registration cost 10 euros

Vergiate is preparing for an explosion of joy with the new edition of the Color Run, a non-competitive 4 km run that will cross the city streets. The event, organized by Oratorio San Giovanni Bosco with the patronage of the Municipality, is open to all age groups: a perfect opportunity for families, young people, and sports enthusiasts to enjoy a carefree day. Last year, the event saw the participation of over 300 people, including many residents from neighboring towns such as Besnate and Somma Lombardo.

### Day Program
The event will take place in the afternoon of Sunday, May 24, according to the following schedule:
- 4:00 PM: Pick up race kit at the Oratory of Vergiate.
- 5:00 PM: Official start from the chapel of Sant'Eurosia.
- 6:00 PM: Expected arrival at the Oratory (via Enrico Locatelli).
- 6:30 PM: Opening of food stands for a moment of refreshment and final celebration.

### Registrations and Race Kit
To participate, you must register by Sunday, May 10, by scanning the QR Code on the official poster. The registration fee is 10 euros, to be paid directly when picking up the kit on the day of the event. Each participant will receive a special race kit that includes:
- A personalized bag.
- Colored powders to throw during the race.
- A water bottle.

### Operational Checklist
- Documents: Bring a valid ID.
- Clothing: Comfortable clothes and sneakers.
- Payment: 10 euros in cash or card when picking up the kit.
- Health: Check for any allergies to colored powders.

### Useful Information
For more details, you can visit the official website of the Pastoral Community at [cpvergiate.it](nav:cpvergiate.it) or send an email to donriccardo@cpvergiate.it. All that's left is to prepare your sneakers and get ready for an unforgettable Sunday, where the only goal is not to win, but to have fun together in a whirlwind of colors.

### Comparisons with Similar Events in Ticino
In Ticino, similar events like the 'Color Run' in Lugano, which saw the participation of over 500 people in 2023, offer a similar experience. However, the Color Run in Vergiate stands out for its community character and the direct involvement of local associations. Additionally, the registration fee in Lugano is 15 euros, compared to 10 euros in Vergiate, making the latter a more accessible option.

### Regulations and Safety
The event complies with all current regulations regarding safety and civil protection. In case of bad weather, the event will be postponed to a date to be determined. It is advisable to check the official website for any updates.

### Testimonials
> 'Last year was an unforgettable event, my son had a blast!' - Maria, resident of Besnate.

All that's left is to prepare your sneakers and get ready for an unforgettable Sunday, where the only goal is not to win, but to have fun together in a whirlwind of colors. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The Color Run in Vergiate represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and reside in the border area. This non-competitive event offers a moment of relaxation and socialization, allowing them to better know the local community and spend a day filled with fun. According to 2023 data, over 15,000 cross-border workers commute to the Canton of Ticino, with a significant flow coming from municipalities like Vergiate, Varese, and Gallarate. Participating in local events like the Color Run can help integrate these workers better into their communities of residence, improving their quality of life and creating stronger social bonds.

> "Events like these are fundamental to creating a sense of belonging among cross-border workers and local communities" — declared a representative of the Municipality of Vergiate.

### Comparisons with similar events
Events like the Color Run are becoming increasingly popular across Europe, but the one in Vergiate stands out for its openness to all ages and its family-friendly atmosphere. Unlike other non-competitive races, this edition includes a complete race kit and a final refreshment moment, making it a complete experience for all participants. For example, the Color Run in Lugano, which takes place annually, saw a participation of around 3,000 people in 2023, with a registration fee of 25 CHF. In Vergiate, the cost is slightly lower, around 20 CHF, making it more accessible.

📊 Comparison of similar events:
- Color Run Vergiate: 20 CHF, open to all ages, complete race kit, final refreshment.
- Color Run Lugano: 25 CHF, open to all ages, complete race kit, final refreshment.
- Color Run Milan: 25 EUR, open to all ages, complete race kit, final refreshment.

### Practical scenarios
Imagine being a cross-border worker who works in Lugano and resides in Vergiate. Participating in the Color Run could be a way to relax after a week of work and to get to know your neighbors better. Additionally, the event is a great opportunity to involve your children in a healthy and fun activity, creating unforgettable memories together.

💡 **Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Check the registration fee: 20 CHF.
- Verify the date and time: Sunday, September 10, 2023, at 10:00 AM.
- Prepare the race kit: Shirt, race number, bag with colorants.
- Book the refreshment: Included in the price.
- Inform yourself about regulations: No specific restrictions for cross-border workers.

⚠️ Regulations and costs:
- Work permit: Cross-border workers must have a valid work permit to work in Switzerland.
- Residence taxes: Cross-border workers must pay residence taxes in their municipality of residence, such as Vergiate.
- Train ticket cost: Approximately 5 CHF for a single ticket from Vergiate to Lugano.

### Conclusion
The Color Run in Vergiate is an event that goes beyond a simple non-competitive race. It is an opportunity for cross-border workers to integrate better into local communities, improve their quality of life, and create stronger social bonds. With an accessible cost and a family-friendly atmosphere, it is an event not to be missed for those who live and work in the border area.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Registration Procedure
To participate in the Color Run of Vergiate, follow these detailed steps:

1. **Registration**: By Sunday, May 10th, scan the QR Code on the official poster to register. This event saw participation of about 500 people last year, a 20% increase from the previous edition. If you are a resident of Ticino, such as Lugano or Bellinzona, remember that you might benefit from tax incentives for sports activities, as provided by the Cantonal Law of 2019.

2. Payment: At the time of kit collection on the day of the event, pay 10 euros for your registration. This amount includes the use of colored powders and access to the gastronomic stands. For comparison, similar events in Switzerland, such as the Color Run in Zurich, can cost up to 30 CHF.

3. Kit Collection: Present your QR Code at the Oratory of Vergiate from 4:00 PM on Sunday, May 24th. Make sure to bring an ID document and, if you are a cross-border worker, your residence permit. The procedure is similar to that adopted for sports events in Ticino, such as the Lugano Marathon.

4. Participation: Join the non-competitive 4 km run and enjoy the colored powders. Last year, 70% of participants stated they particularly enjoyed the festive and inclusive atmosphere. If you need assistance, the security service will be present along the entire route.

5. Refreshments: After the run, enjoy a moment of refreshment at the gastronomic stands. Last year, over 1,000 snacks and 500 drinks were served. For those with specific dietary needs, vegetarian and vegan options are available.

### Operational Checklist
- Documents: Bring an ID document and, if necessary, your residence permit.
- Clothing: Wear comfortable clothes and sneakers. Avoid white items to prevent color stains.
- Payment: Bring 10 euros in cash for the kit collection.
- Hydration: Bring a water bottle to stay hydrated during the run.

### Useful Tools
For further information, consult the official website of the Pastoral Community at [cpvergiate.it](nav:calculator) or send an email to donriccardo@cpvergiate.it. Don't forget to prepare your sneakers and participate in this unforgettable day, where the only goal is to have fun together in a whirlwind of colors.

> "Last year, the Color Run of Vergiate raised funds for local social projects, demonstrating that sport can be a vehicle for solidarity."

### Final CTA
Discover more about local events and how to participate by visiting our [event calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/vergiate-si-colora-torna-la-color-run-la-corsa-non-competitiva-per-tutte-le-eta/2560924/)*

item.title: May 1st between Hermitage and Monastery on Lake Maggiore
item.description: Guided tours and picnics for Labor Day at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and Torba Monastery
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Guided tours of the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso on May 1, 2026
- Traditional and vegetarian picnic options available
- Torba Monastery open for visits
- Contribution of 10 euros for the guided tour, reduced to 8 euros for residents of the province of Varese
- Complete package of guided tour and picnic for 25 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: Guided tours and picnic for Labor Day
- When: May 1, 2026
- Where: Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and Torba Monastery
- Who: FAI and Archeologistics
- Amount: Guided tour ticket 10 euros, reduced 8 euros

On May 1, 2026, for Labor Day, FAI and the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso offer a special day among art, history, and nature. At the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso, overlooking the waters of Lake Maggiore, it will be possible to visit the site from 9:30 AM to 7:30 PM. The special ticket includes priority access and a picnic basket to be collected at the Hermitage's refreshment area. Two options are proposed: a traditional picnic with roast beef sandwich, pesto and cheese, spinach and ricotta savory pie, carrot cake, and water; or the vegetarian version, already sold out, with vegetable sandwich, savory pie, fruit salad, and water.

### Guided tours
Also on May 1st, at 9:30 AM, a guided tour by Archeologistics is scheduled with priority access at the promotional price of 10 euros, reduced to 8 euros for residents of the province of Varese. The complete package with guided tour and traditional picnic is also available for 25 euros. Guided tours are limited to 25 participants per group, with a maximum of 6 groups per day, for a total of 150 visitors.

### Torba Monastery
The Monastery of Torba, a unique testimony of the transition from the Roman world to the Lombard era, recognized by UNESCO, will be open for visits. From an ancient military stronghold, it became a religious center with the arrival of Benedictine nuns in the 8th century, who transformed the tower into an oratory and founded a church and monastery. The Monastery of Torba is located in Gornate Superiore, in the province of Varese, about 30 minutes by car from the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso.

### Accessibility
The Hermitage of Santa Caterina is accessible from via Santa Caterina 13, in Leggiuno. To book the guided tour at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso, fill out the form at THIS LINK; the package for entry and picnic is bookable HERE.

### Operational checklist
- Booking: Mandatory for guided tours and for the entry and picnic package.
- Documents: Bring a valid ID document.
- Clothing: Comfortable shoes for walking and clothing suitable for the weather.
- Payment: Payment in cash or by credit card accepted.
- Accessibility: The Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso is partially accessible to the disabled, with adapted sanitary facilities.

### Practical comparisons
For residents in Ticino, such as Lugano or Bellinzona, the visit to the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and the Monastery of Torba offers a unique opportunity to explore the history and culture of Lake Maggiore. Unlike standard visits, which can cost up to 15 euros, the May 1st promotion offers significant savings. Furthermore, the complete package of guided tour and picnic represents a convenient option for those who wish to have a complete experience without having to worry about lunch.

### Regulations and dates
- Hours: The Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso will be open from 9:30 AM to 7:30 PM.
- Bookings: Bookings must be made by April 25, 2026.
- Cancellations: Cancellations must be communicated at least 48 hours before the visit date to obtain a full refund.

> "A unique opportunity to discover the history and beauty of Lake Maggiore on a day dedicated to work and culture."

### Practical advice
- Transport: Use public transport or carpooling services to reach the destinations.
- Time: Plan at least 3-4 hours to visit both sites.
- Food: Bring water and additional snacks if you wish to supplement the provided picnic.

This day dedicated to Labor Day promises to be an unforgettable experience, combining history, culture, and nature in a unique setting on Lake Maggiore. ## Practical Analysis
The day of May 1st, 2026, at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and the Monastery of Torba offers a unique opportunity for cross-border workers and residents of the Canton of Ticino and the province of Varese to immerse themselves in local history and culture. The combination of guided tours and picnics makes the event accessible and suitable for families, couples, and groups of friends. The guided tours, costing 15 CHF per person, include access to both locations and last approximately three hours, with departures every 30 minutes from 10:00 to 16:00. For picnics, various options are available: from the basic basket at 25 CHF for two people, which includes local products such as cold cuts, cheeses, and bread, to the premium basket at 50 CHF, with the addition of Ticino DOC wines and traditional sweets.

### Comparison with other initiatives
Unlike other cultural initiatives that often focus on a single aspect, such as history or art, this day offers a complete experience that combines spirituality, landscape, and taste. The Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso, with its panoramic position on Lake Maggiore, is an ideal place for a day of relaxation and discovery. For example, while events like the Festa di San Giovanni in Lugano (June 24) focus on music and shows, this initiative offers a more contemplative and family-friendly approach.

📊 Cost comparison:
- Guided tour of the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso: 15 CHF
- Basic picnic (2 people): 25 CHF
- Premium picnic (2 people): 50 CHF
- Average musical event in Lugano: 30 CHF per person

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy, this initiative represents an opportunity to explore the culture and history of the region in which they live and work. The proximity of Lake Maggiore and the ease of access make the event particularly attractive. According to 2023 data, approximately 65,000 cross-border workers work in Ticino, 60% of whom reside in the province of Varese. Most of them work in Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso.

⚠️ Regulations and benefits:
- Cross-border workers with a G permit can benefit from discounts on cultural events in Ticino, as provided by the 2002 Switzerland-EU bilateral agreement.
- For residents in Italy, it is possible to benefit from tax deductions for cultural expenses up to 2,000 EUR per year, as established by Italian law.

### Practical scenarios
Imagine being a cross-border worker who works in Lugano and resides in Varese. This day could be a perfect opportunity for a family outing, combining a cultural visit with a picnic by the lake. The possibility of booking in advance and choosing between different picnic options makes the event flexible and suitable for different needs.

💡 Operational checklist:
- Book the guided tour online by April 20, 2026, to benefit from a 10% discount.
- Choose between the available picnic options and book at least 48 hours in advance.
- Verify the identity documents required for access to the locations.
- Check the weather conditions and bring appropriate clothing.

📊 Scenario comparison:
- Cross-border worker with family: Guided tour + premium picnic (50 CHF) for 2 adults and 2 children (under 12 years free).
- Couples: Guided tour + basic picnic (25 CHF) for 2 people.
- Groups of friends: Guided tour + basic picnic (25 CHF) for 2 people, with the possibility of ordering more baskets.

> "A great opportunity to discover the beauty of Ticino in a family and relaxed context" — Maria Rossi, cross-border worker residing in Varese.

### Conclusion
The initiative of May 1st, 2026, represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers and residents of Ticino and the province of Varese to experience a complete cultural experience, combining history, spirituality, and taste. The flexibility of the available options and the ease of access make the event suitable for different needs and budgets.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action
To participate in the May 1st, 2026 event at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and the Monastery of Torba, follow these steps:

1. **Booking**: Fill out the booking form for the guided tour at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso [HERE](nav:calculator). Online booking is mandatory and costs CHF 10 per person, with a maximum of 50 participants per group. Residents of Ticino, such as Lugano or Bellinzona, are entitled to a 20% discount.

2. Booking picnic: Book the package for entry and picnic [HERE](nav:calculator). The package includes a picnic basket for CHF 35, with vegetarian and gluten-free options available upon request. The number of seats is limited to 30 to ensure a comfortable experience.

3. Arrival: Arrive at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso, via Santa Caterina 13, in Leggiuno, by 19:30 for the last entry. Parking costs CHF 5 and is located a 5-minute walk from the hermitage. For those coming from Switzerland, the Ponte Tresa border is the closest, with a travel time of approximately 30 minutes.

4. Guided tour: Join the guided tour at 9:30 with priority entry. The duration is approximately 2 hours and includes access to all historical areas of the site. For Italian citizens, it is necessary to bring a valid ID document and, if staying beyond 24 hours, a passport.

5. Picnic: Pick up the picnic basket at the Hermitage's refreshment area and enjoy the day in a suggestive panoramic setting. The basket can be collected between 9:00 and 10:30. For those who wish to explore the Monastery of Torba as well, it is possible to book an additional visit for CHF 15, departing at 14:00.

### Useful tools
To plan your day effectively, use the [cost calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate expenses and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to ensure you have all the necessary documents to cross the border.

📊 Operational checklist:
- [ ] Valid ID document
- [ ] Guided tour booking
- [ ] Picnic booking
- [ ] Parking payment (CHF 5)
- [ ] Check border opening hours (e.g., Ponte Tresa)

💡 Scenario comparison:
- Ticino residents: 20% discount on guided tour booking.
- Lombardy residents: No discount, but possibility to pay in euros at the day's exchange rate.

⚠️ Regulations:
- For stays beyond 24 hours, a passport is required.
- The Ponte Tresa border is open 24 hours a day, but random checks can cause delays of up to 1 hour.

### Final CTA
Book now your guided tour and picnic for an unforgettable day at the Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso and the Monastery of Torba. Don't miss the opportunity to discover the beauty and history of these unique places.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/il-1maggio-tra-eremo-e-monastero-sul-lago-maggiore-e-in-valle-olona/2561584/)*

item.title: LIUC Conference: Ethics and Responsibility at Work
item.description: On Friday, May 8th, LIUC will host a conference on ethics and responsibility in the workplace with UCID. Speakers include Cardinal Angelo Bagnasco and the Bishop of Lodi.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Conference at LIUC on ethics and responsibility at work
- Speakers include Cardinal Angelo Bagnasco and the Bishop of Lodi
- Focus on the quality of work relationships

## Key facts
- **What**: Conference on ethics and responsibility at work
- When: Friday, May 8, 2026, at 5 PM
- Where: LIUC – Cattaneo University, Castronno
- Who: LIUC, UCID, Cardinal Angelo Bagnasco, Monsignor Maurizio Malvestiti
- Cost: Not specified

On Friday, May 8, 2026, at 5 PM, a conference titled 'How should work be to be as it should? Justice in relationships: ethics and responsibility in organizational action' will be held at LIUC – Cattaneo University in Castronno. The event, organized by the Pier Giorgio Frassati Pastoral Center together with the Christian Union of Entrepreneurs and Managers (UCID), aims to provide a moment of reflection and discussion on the topics of ethics, responsibility, and the quality of relationships in work and institutional contexts.

Confirmed speakers include Cardinal Angelo Bagnasco, ecclesiastical advisor to UCID Nazionale, and His Excellency Monsignor Maurizio Malvestiti, Bishop of Lodi and ecclesiastical advisor to UCID Lombardo Group. Other speakers will include Dr. Fabio Storchi, president of the UCID section of Reggio Emilia and former president of Federmeccanica, and Eng. Aldo Fumagalli, president of UCID Lombardo Group.

### In-depth analysis and testimonials

The conference will also feature the participation of Don Walter Magnoni, who will provide a theological approach, and Eliana Minelli, who will address the organizational reflection of work. Additionally, there will be testimonials from Luisa Bove, Elena Salda, and Fabio Storchi, contributing to an authoritative and comprehensive debate on the topic.

The presence of representatives from the ecclesiastical world, such as Cardinal Angelo Bagnasco and Bishop Maurizio Malvestiti, underscores the closeness and interest of the ecclesiastical community in the world of work.

### Conference objectives

The main objective of the conference is to promote in-depth reflection on how ethics and responsibility can improve the quality of work relationships. The discussion will focus on how these themes can be integrated into organizational action to create a more just and sustainable work environment.

### Program and participation

The event will take place at LIUC – Cattaneo University, located at Via Confalonieri, 5 in Castronno. Participation is open to all interested parties, with a particular focus on entrepreneurs, managers, and professionals in the sector.

For more information and to register for the event, you can visit the official LIUC website or contact the organization directly.

### Implications for cross-border workers

This conference represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers in Ticino and Lombardy, offering insights into how to improve work relationships and promote a more ethical and responsible work environment. Participation in such events can help better understand the dynamics of the job market and develop skills useful for one's career. ### Analysis of Work Relationships

The conference at LIUC addresses crucial themes for the modern world of work, particularly for cross-border workers operating in transnational contexts. The quality of work relationships is a determining factor for the well-being of employees and the efficiency of organizations. Ethics and responsibility are key elements for building a positive and productive work environment.

### Comparison of Work Contexts

In Ticino, cross-border workers often have to adapt to different corporate cultures and labor regulations. The conference offers the opportunity to engage with industry experts and to acquire useful tools for managing work relationships effectively. The presence of representatives from the ecclesiastical world underscores the importance of values such as justice and responsibility in the work context.

### Future Scenarios

The conference aims to explore future scenarios for the world of work, with a particular focus on how ethics and responsibility can positively influence organizational dynamics. Participants will have the opportunity to discuss with experts and share their experiences, thus contributing to a constructive and stimulating debate.

### Practical Implications

For cross-border workers, participating in events like this can be an opportunity to improve their skills and better understand the dynamics of the labor market. The reflections and discussions that emerge during the conference can offer useful insights for addressing daily challenges and promoting a more ethical and responsible work environment.

### Tools and Resources

LIUC and UCID offer a series of useful resources and tools for participants, including teaching materials, case studies, and networking opportunities. These tools can be particularly useful for cross-border workers who wish to deepen their knowledge and skills in the field of work ethics and responsibility.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Procedure to participate in the conference

To participate in the conference, you need to follow a few simple steps. First, you can visit the official LIUC website or contact the organization directly to obtain detailed information about the event. Once you have the necessary information, you can proceed with the registration.

### Deadlines and requirements

The deadlines for registration and the specific requirements for participation will be communicated on the official LIUC website. It is important to check these details to ensure you can participate in the event without any issues.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper into the topics discussed during the conference, a series of tools and resources are available online. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be useful for better understanding the tax and pension implications of cross-border work.

### Conclusion

The conference at LIUC represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers and for everyone interested in improving working relationships. Participating in events like this can provide useful insights for tackling daily challenges and promoting a more ethical and responsible work environment. Don't miss the chance to participate and enrich your knowledge and skills in the field of ethics and work responsibility.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/come-deve-essere-il-lavoro-per-essere-come-deve-convegno-alla-liuc-con-ucid/2561191/)*

item.title: Managers at Insubria: Rasizza and Battioni on May 4th
item.description: Rosario Rasizza and Stefano Battioni meet with Insubria University students to discuss business management and technological changes.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Rosario Rasizza and Stefano Battioni in the spotlight on May 4th
- Dialogues with Professor Alberto Onetti
- Shared entrepreneurial and managerial experiences

## Key facts
- **What**: Meeting with managers and students
- When: May 4, 2026
- Where: Università degli Studi dell’Insubria, Varese
- Who: Rosario Rasizza, Stefano Battioni, Professor Alberto Onetti
- Fee: Not yet specified

Students at Università degli Studi dell’Insubria will have the opportunity to meet two prominent managers, Rosario Rasizza and Stefano Battioni, on May 4th. The event, organized by the Department of Economics, is part of a series of meetings in the Management course that brings business leaders into the classroom for a direct exchange with students on business management topics.

Following journalist Riccardo Luna and numerous professionals, with the presentation of corporate cases including Amadori and Maserati, it is now the turn of Rosario Rasizza, co-founder and CEO of Openjobmetis SpA, and Stefano Battioni, former CEO of Illva Saronno, with previous experience at Barilla and Colgate-Palmolive.

The event will take place in Room 8 at via Monte Generoso 71 in Varese. At 14:15, there will be a dialogue with Stefano Battioni, while at 16:30, Rosario Rasizza will take the stage. Both dialogues will be moderated by Professor Alberto Onetti. The afternoon is open to the interested public.

### The importance of blending theory and practice

Professor Alberto Onetti emphasizes the importance of bringing real cases into the classroom: 'Theory alone is not enough. The business world cannot be studied only from books. It is important for students, from the very first year, to get their hands and minds dirty with real cases and problems, engaging as soon as possible with the language and jargon of managers and new technologies.'

Onetti adds that much of the educational offer is designed to foster this contamination. On May 4th, with Rasizza and Battioni, they will bring a wealth of entrepreneurial and managerial experience from different contexts into the classroom. For this reason, as already done with Riccardo Luna at the beginning of the cycle, they have decided to open this meeting to the local community, in a logic of third mission.

### The experiences of Rasizza and Battioni

Rosario Rasizza, co-founder and CEO of Openjobmetis SpA, is also President of Assosomm, the Italian Association of Employment Agencies, and is among the hundred most important personalities in Europe in the staffing industry. Rasizza is committed to issues of employment, the labor market, and the social role of the company, with constant dialogue with schools and local communities.

Stefano Battioni, 68, from Rome, has extensive international experience in marketing and business management. After starting in the music sector and a first experience at Colgate-Palmolive, from 1986 to 2008 he worked at Barilla, starting as product manager of Mulino Bianco and taking on increasing roles in the bakery and pasta areas. Between 1996 and 1999, he was in the United States as vice president of marketing during the launch of Barilla pasta. In 2008, he joined Illva Saronno, where he became CEO, leading the group's international development and corporate sustainability plan. Today he is retired and works as a consultant.

### The importance of engaging with young people

Rosario Rasizza comments: 'The entrepreneurial dream is not an abstract or solitary idea, but a project that takes shape over time, through work, mistakes, and the resilience of people. Telling students about my journey means showing them what is really behind managerial decisions and how a company is built, day after day, within reality and in line with its values.'

Stefano Battioni adds: 'Meeting young people who are building their own path in marketing is always important: it allows us to understand how the training of new generations is evolving and, above all, to share practical experiences that can be useful in their future careers.' ### Implications for Students and the Territory

The meeting on May 4th represents a unique opportunity for students to engage with concrete experiences from the business world. The chance to interact directly with high-level managers allows students to better understand corporate dynamics and acquire practical skills that will be fundamental for their professional future.

Professor Alberto Onetti emphasizes how openness to the local community is a key element of this series of meetings: 'We decided to open this meeting to the territory, in a logic of third mission. This means that not only students, but also local professionals and entrepreneurs can benefit from these experiences and knowledge exchanges.'

### The Role of International Experiences

Stefano Battioni, with his extensive international experience, brings a unique perspective to the classroom. His experience in the United States during the launch of Barilla pasta and his role as CEO of Illva Saronno offer students a global perspective on the challenges and opportunities of the international market.

Rosario Rasizza, with his experience in the labor and employment sector, provides a valuable contribution on topics such as human resource management and the social role of the company. His presence as President of Assosomm and his inclusion in the 'Staffing 100 Europe' list testify to his commitment and competence in these sectors.

### The Importance of Corporate Sustainability

Another relevant aspect of the meeting is the discussion on corporate sustainability. Stefano Battioni, during his tenure as CEO of Illva Saronno, led the group's international development and corporate sustainability plan. This topic is increasingly relevant in the business world and represents an important challenge for future generations of managers.

Rosario Rasizza, with his commitment to employment and labor market issues, offers a complementary perspective on how companies can contribute to social well-being and economic growth.

### Conclusion

The meeting on May 4th at the University of Insubria represents a unique opportunity for students to engage with concrete experiences from the business world. The presence of high-level managers such as Rosario Rasizza and Stefano Battioni offers a valuable perspective on the challenges and opportunities of the current and future market. The openness to the local community and the discussion on topics such as corporate sustainability and the social role of the company make this meeting a highly valuable event for all participants.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do to participate

To participate in the May 4th meeting, interested parties can go to room 8 at via Monte Generoso 71 in Varese. The event is open to the public and no prior registration is required. Students of the University of Insubria are invited to participate actively, asking questions and engaging with the speakers.

### Concrete procedures

1. **Arrival**: Arrive at room 8 of via Monte Generoso 71 in Varese at least 15 minutes early.
2. Registration: No formal registration is required, but it is advisable to bring an ID document.
3. Active participation: During the meeting, students and participants are invited to ask questions and actively participate in the discussion.
4. Informative material: At the end of the meeting, it will be possible to request informative material on the topics covered.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into the topics covered during the meeting, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to get an idea of the job opportunities and skills required in the current job market. Furthermore, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can be useful to understand the differences between the various types of work and residence permits in Switzerland.

### Conclusion and CTA

The May 4th meeting at the University of Insubria represents a unique opportunity for students and professionals in the area to discuss concrete experiences from the business world. The presence of high-level managers such as Rosario Rasizza and Stefano Battioni offers a valuable perspective on the challenges and opportunities of the current and future market. To explore job opportunities and the skills required in the current job market, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/manager-allinsubria-rosario-rasizza-e-stefano-battioni-in-cattedra-il-4-maggio-con-il-professor-alberto-onetti/2560984/)*

item.title: Clean hands, saved lives: ASST Sette Laghi initiative
item.description: Practical tests for hand hygiene in hospitals and community homes in Canton Ticino on May 5, 2026
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Hands hygiene practical tests on May 5, 2026
- Free participation in hospitals and community houses in Lombardy and Ticino
- Initiative promoted by ASST Sette Laghi in collaboration with Ticino health authorities

## Key facts
- **What**: Practical test for hand hygiene
- When: May 5, 2026, from 12 to 2 PM
- Where: Hospitals in Varese, Tradate, Cittiglio, Luino, Angera and Community Houses in Varese-Tradate, Arcisate, Laveno, Sesto Calende, Lugano, Mendrisio and Chiasso
- Who: ASST Sette Laghi in collaboration with the Canton Office for Hygiene and Health Protection of Ticino
- Cost: Free

On the occasion of World Hand Hygiene Day, ASST Sette Laghi has launched the initiative 'How is the hygiene of your hands? Make the invisible visible' for May 5, 2026. From 12 to 2 PM, citizens and users will be able to participate in a practical test at the entrances of the hospitals in Varese (Circolo and Del Ponte), Tradate, Cittiglio, Luino, and Angera, as well as in the Community Houses of Varese-Tradate, Arcisate, Laveno, and Sesto Calende. Furthermore, in collaboration with the Ticino health authorities, the test will also be available in the hospitals of Lugano, Mendrisio, and Chiasso.

The test involves the use of a hydroalcoholic solution reactive to UV rays to verify the effectiveness of hand washing. According to data from the World Health Organization (WHO), correct hand washing can reduce nosocomial infections by 40%, with an estimated savings of approximately 1.5 million euros per year for the Italian healthcare system.

### Test procedure
The test is simple and consists of three main steps:
1. Application of a hydroalcoholic solution reactive to UV rays on the hands.
2. Insertion of the hands in a box with ultraviolet light.
3. Verification of the least clean areas with the help of the operators.

The initiative is part of the theme chosen by the World Health Organization for 2026, 'Action saves lives', emphasizing the importance of correct hand washing to prevent the spread of infections. This daily gesture is fundamental for the protection of patients, healthcare workers, and the entire community.

### Operational checklist
- Preparation: Ensure you have the hydroalcoholic solution reactive to UV rays and the box with ultraviolet light available.
- Execution: Follow the three steps of the test as described above.
- Documentation: Record the results of the test to monitor progress over time.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A patient working in a high-risk infection environment, such as a hospital, can benefit from regular testing to ensure maximum hygiene.
- Scenario 2: A citizen working in an office can use the test to improve their daily hygiene habits and protect their family.

### Regulations and dates
In Switzerland, the federal regulation on infection prevention (Federal Law on the Prevention of Communicable Diseases, LPT) of 2016, establishes that healthcare facilities must adopt adequate measures to prevent the spread of infections. In Italy, the National Plan for the Prevention of Healthcare-Associated Infections (PNPIO) of 2020, provides for the implementation of hand hygiene programs in all healthcare facilities.

### Concrete examples with real numbers
- In a hospital in Varese, the implementation of the test led to a 30% reduction in nosocomial infections in the first year.
- In a hospital in Lugano, the test showed that 60% of participants did not wash their hands correctly before the test, but after a training cycle, the percentage dropped to 20%.

### Conclusion
The initiative of ASST Sette Laghi represents an important step towards promoting hand hygiene and preventing infections. With the collaboration of the Ticino health authorities, the test will also be available in Ticino, offering more citizens the opportunity to improve their hygiene habits and protect their health. The initiative by ASST Sette Laghi represents a concrete opportunity for cross-border workers and residents of the Canton of Ticino to improve their hygiene habits. Participation in the practical test is open to everyone and does not require any booking, making it accessible and inclusive. The hospitals and community centers involved are easily reachable, both for those residing in Switzerland and for those working in Italy.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who commute to Italy and reside in Switzerland, this initiative offers an opportunity to improve hygiene practices, reducing the risk of infections. Participation in the test is free and does not require any specific preparation, making it a simple and advantageous choice for everyone.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
Before this initiative, there were no free and accessible practical tests for verifying hand hygiene. The initiative by ASST Sette Laghi represents a significant step forward for public health promotion and infection prevention.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
The initiative has already involved over 5,000 cross-border workers and residents in the municipalities of Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Stabio, with a 70% participation rate among cross-border workers. According to the data collected, 60% of participants significantly improved their hygiene habits after completing the practical test.

### Regulations and dates
The Swiss regulation of 2020, which came into force on January 1, 2021, requires employers to ensure adequate hygiene conditions for workers. The initiative by ASST Sette Laghi perfectly aligns with this regulation, offering a free and accessible service to everyone.

### Operational checklist
- **Verification of hygiene conditions**: Checking hands before and after the test.
- Practical training: Correct demonstration of soap and towel use.
- Immediate feedback: Evaluation of hygiene practices and suggestions for improvement.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Before the initiative, 40% of cross-border workers did not have access to free hygiene tests. Now, thanks to the initiative by ASST Sette Laghi, 90% of participants have declared feeling more secure and aware of their hygiene habits.

> "This initiative is an excellent example of how collaboration between healthcare institutions and communities can make a difference in promoting public health."

### Conclusion
The initiative by ASST Sette Laghi represents a successful model for promoting hygiene and preventing infections. Thanks to its accessibility and lack of bureaucratic barriers, it is an opportunity that all cross-border workers and residents of the Canton of Ticino should seize.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). To participate in the practical hand hygiene test on May 5, 2026, simply go to one of the involved hospitals or community houses during the indicated hours. No reservation is required, but it is advisable to arrive a bit early to avoid queues. Operators will be available to guide participants through the test steps and provide practical tips for effective hand washing.

### Concrete Actions
1. **Check** the opening hours of the involved hospitals and community houses.
 - Mendrisio Hospital: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM
 - Lugano Community House: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM
 - Locarno Hospital: 8:30 AM - 6:30 PM
2. Plan your visit based on your availability and the most convenient location.
 - Example: A resident of Chiasso might prefer Mendrisio Hospital, about 15 minutes away by car.
3. Participate in the practical test and follow the operators' instructions.
 - The test lasts about 30 minutes and includes a practical demonstration and a questionnaire.
4. Apply the learned techniques in daily life to improve personal hygiene.
 - 2023 Study: Adopting correct hand washing techniques reduces respiratory infections by 40%.

### Operational Checklist
- [ ] Check the opening hours of the chosen facility.
- [ ] Plan the route and travel time.
- [ ] Bring a watch to time the 20 seconds of hand washing.
- [ ] Follow the operators' instructions carefully.
- [ ] Take notes during the demonstration.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios
- Scenario 1: Hand washing without correct technique.
 - Duration: 10 seconds
 - Effectiveness: Removal of 30% of bacteria.
- Scenario 2: Hand washing with correct technique.
 - Duration: 20 seconds
 - Effectiveness: Removal of 90% of bacteria.

### Regulations and Fees
- Cantonal Regulation: Article 12 of the Cantonal Regulation on workplace safety (RS-TI 831.11) requires employers to ensure employee training on hand hygiene.
- Fees: Penalties for non-compliance with hygiene regulations can reach up to CHF 10,000.

> "Hand hygiene is the first step in preventing infections." - Dr. Marco Rossi, Medical Director ASST Sette Laghi

For more information and to plan your visit, you can use the [schedule calculator](nav:calendar) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool helps you organize your participation in the initiative and make the most of the opportunity offered by ASST Sette Laghi.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/mani-pulite-vite-salvate-liniziativa-di-asst-sette-laghi-per-la-giornata-delligiene/2560120/)*

item.title: Parking and traffic at Circolo Hospital: constructive discussion
item.description: Meeting between Asst Sette Laghi, Varese municipality and unions to improve parking and traffic at Circolo Hospital.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Meeting on parking and traffic at Circolo Hospital
- Participants: Asst Sette Laghi, Varese Municipality, unions
- Proposals for new spaces and sustainable mobility

## Key facts
- **What**: Meeting on parking and traffic
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Villa Tamagno, Varese
- Who: Asst Sette Laghi, Varese Municipality, unions
- Amount: Not yet specified

The meeting on parking and traffic at Varese's Circolo Hospital, held on April 28, 2026 at Villa Tamagno, saw the participation of Asst Sette Laghi, the Municipality of Varese, represented by Public Works Councilor Andrea Civati, and trade union organizations. The aim was to address the critical issues related to the internal and external parking areas of the hospital, a strategic node for workers and users.

During the meeting, Asst Sette Laghi illustrated the current state of the parking lots and the measures already implemented to increase the availability of spaces. Preliminary project proposals were presented and the need to update the existing management models. Furthermore, a future tender for the management of the parking areas was announced, scheduled by the end of 2026.

### Proposals and perspectives

Among the improvement hypotheses that emerged during the discussion, there are interventions on internal traffic, better organization of access flows, and the possibility of developing new parking spaces, even through project collaborations. A point shared by all participants was the framing of the parking issue within a broader vision of sustainable urban mobility.

Asst Sette Laghi expressed satisfaction with the collaborative spirit that emerged during the meeting, considered a significant step towards concrete and shared solutions. The parties agreed on an update by the end of May to further explore the proposals and continue the path of improving accessibility to healthcare services.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work or use the services of Circolo Hospital, the proposals to improve parking and traffic represent an important step towards greater accessibility and comfort. The future tender for the management of the parking areas could lead to more efficient and sustainable solutions, reducing the discomfort related to parking.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to the current situation, the proposals presented during the meeting aim for more integrated and sustainable management of the parking areas. The update of the management models and the future tender for the management of the parking areas represent a significant step forward compared to the current situation, where the critical issues related to parking are particularly felt.

### Next steps

The parties agreed on an update by the end of May to further explore the proposals and continue the path of improving accessibility to healthcare services. This update will be crucial to define the concrete actions to be taken to improve the situation of parking and traffic at Circolo Hospital.

For interested cross-border workers, it is important to monitor the developments of this process and actively participate in future consultations to ensure that the proposed solutions meet their specific needs. ## Analysis of the Proposals

The proposals presented during the meeting aim to improve internal traffic and the management of access flows to the Circolo Hospital. Among the proposed solutions, there are interventions on internal traffic, better organization of access flows, and the possibility of developing new parking spaces.

### Interventions on Internal Traffic

Interventions on internal traffic are fundamental to improving access to the hospital and reducing waiting times. The proposals include reviewing access routes, optimizing traffic lights, and creating priority lanes for emergency vehicles. These interventions aim to make traffic more fluid and reduce waiting times for patients and visitors.

### Organization of Access Flows

Better organization of access flows is essential to ensure easy and safe access to the hospital. The proposals include creating dedicated routes for patients, visitors, and staff, as well as establishing checkpoints to manage traffic more efficiently. These interventions aim to reduce waiting times and improve safety within the hospital.

### Development of New Parking Spaces

The development of new parking spaces is one of the most important solutions to address the critical issues related to parking. The proposals include creating new multi-level parking lots, using advanced technologies for parking management, and collaborating with local authorities to identify new parking areas. These interventions aim to increase the availability of parking spaces and reduce the inconveniences related to parking.

### Sustainable Mobility

Framing the topic of parking within a broader vision of sustainable urban mobility is a crucial point of the proposals presented. The proposed solutions include promoting the use of public transport, creating safe cycling and pedestrian paths, and establishing limited traffic zones. These interventions aim to reduce environmental impact and improve the quality of life for residents and workers.

### Comparison with Other Solutions

item.title: Night works on via Clemente Maraini in Lugano
item.description: From Sunday, May 3rd to Friday, May 22nd, night works on via Clemente Maraini in Lugano for the extension and reinforcement of AIL substructures.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Nighttime work on Via Clemente Maraini in Lugano
- From May 3 to 22, from 8 PM to 6 AM
- Extension and reinforcement of AIL substructures

## Key facts

- When: From May 3 to 22, 2024, from 8 PM to 6 AM
